suggested_cluster_label: Thin-film solid-state battery fabrication via sputter deposition
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms (electrode, electrolyte, substrate, cathode, plasma, deposition, target, energy storage) and title terms (energy storage, sputter deposition, cathode, cell) together with abstracts describing stacks of first electrode/electrolyte/second electrode layers on a substrate with grooves and material deposition steps, point clearly to thin-film solid-state electrochemical cell manufacturing using sputter/plasma deposition. Module candidates map directly to the layer types (electrode, electrolyte), the stack architecture, and the deposition process referenced in the evidence.

suggested_cluster_label: Haircare/Hair Styling Appliances with Airflow Attachments
engineering_module_candidates: Airflow generation subsystem (air inlet, air outlet, airflow generator)
Interchangeable styling attachments (e.g., diffuser, handheld attachments)
Attachment aerodynamic surfaces (curved/flat surfaces downstream of air outlet)
rationale: Top TF-IDF and title terms ('haircare appliance', 'hair styling', 'attachment', 'airflow', 'air inlet', 'air outlet', 'diffuser') and representative titles ('HAIRCARE APPLIANCE') indicate the cluster centers on hair styling/drying devices. Abstracts repeatedly describe an airflow generator moving air from inlet to outlet and attachments with specific curved/flat surfaces near the outlet, directly supporting the airflow-generation, attachment, and aerodynamic-surface modules.

suggested_cluster_label: Handheld appliance fluid flow path architecture (e.g., hairdryer airflow ducting)
engineering_module_candidates: Internal duct and flow path routing
Heater assembly integrated in flow path
Attachment interface for handheld appliance body
rationale: Top terms ('fluid', 'fluid flow path', 'fluid inlet', 'fluid outlet', 'duct', 'wall') and title terms ('hand held appliance', 'heater assembly', 'attachment', 'cleaning appliance') combined with abstracts repeatedly describing a hairdryer body with a duct and primary fluid flow path from inlet to outlet point to a cluster centered on the airflow/fluid path architecture of handheld appliances. Heater assembly and attachment terms further support sub-modules for heating and detachable accessories, though 'cleaning appliance' suggests the cluster may extend slightly beyond hairdryers, lowering specificity.

suggested_cluster_label: Sensor-driven smart vacuum cleaners and mobile cleaning robots
engineering_module_candidates: Diagnostic sensor and signal processing module
rationale: Representative titles are dominated by 'VACUUM CLEANER' and top title terms include 'robot', 'mobile robot', and 'robotic', indicating both handheld and mobile robotic vacuums. Abstracts repeatedly describe a 'vacuum motor', 'sensors' generating signals from 'motion and orientation', a 'controller' that processes signals to determine 'surface type', a 'cleaner head comprising an agitator', and 'diagnostic sensors' — directly supporting the listed modules. Terms like 'oral' and 'hairstyling' appear in title terms but are not reflected in the sampled abstracts, so confidence in those as separate themes is low and they are excluded from the modules.

suggested_cluster_label: Vacuum Cleaner Head with Agitator/Brush Bar Assembly
engineering_module_candidates: Agitator/brush bar assembly and drive mechanism
Suction inlet and airflow path to suction generator
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('cleaner head', 'agitator', 'brush bar', 'suction', 'housing', 'chamber') and representative titles uniformly referencing 'CLEANER HEAD FOR A VACUUM CLEANER' indicate a clear focus on the cleaner-head subsystem. Abstracts explicitly describe a housing defining a suction chamber with inlet/outlet, rotating agitator(s) with drive mechanisms, and at least one variant featuring a laser diode for debris illumination, supporting the listed module candidates.

suggested_cluster_label: Surface-treating appliance main body & support/wand architecture
rationale: Top terms ('main body', 'surface treating appliance', 'support assembly', 'hose', 'wand', 'surface treating head') and abstracts repeatedly describe a vacuum-cleaner-type appliance composed of a main body, a rollable support assembly (e.g., dome-shaped wheels), a hose/wand assembly, and a surface treating head with a handle. These structural sub-systems are directly evidenced. The presence of 'robotic' in title terms is minor and not strongly supported by the shown abstracts, so robotic-platform modules are not proposed with confidence.

suggested_cluster_label: Multi-stage cyclonic dirt/dust separation for vacuum cleaners
engineering_module_candidates: First-stage cyclonic separation unit (primary cyclone + dust collection chamber)
Airflow path from dirty-air inlet to clean-air outlet
Handheld cleaning appliance housing integrating the separating apparatus
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('cyclonic separating', 'cyclone', 'dirt', 'dust', 'separator', 'chamber', 'stage', 'vacuum cleaner') and titles like 'MULTISTAGE CYCLONIC SEPARATING APPARATUS' and 'Dirt and Dust Cyclonic Separating Apparatus' directly indicate cyclonic separation technology. Abstracts explicitly describe a first cyclonic separating unit with a cyclone and annular dust collecting chamber, plus a second cyclonic separating unit downstream, and an airflow path from a dirty-air inlet to a clean-air outlet within a handheld cleaning appliance, supporting the listed modules.

engineering_module_candidates: Nozzle airflow module (interior passage and mouth for air emission)
Base impeller-motor module (air inlet, impeller, motor driving airflow)
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('nozzle', 'interior passage', 'mouth', 'impeller', 'air outlet', 'fan assembly') and abstracts explicitly describe a bladeless fan with a base housing an impeller/motor and a nozzle with an interior passage and mouth emitting airflow. Title term 'humidifying' supports a humidification variant as an additional module.

suggested_cluster_label: Brushless (Permanent-Magnet) Motor Control via Winding Excitation
engineering_module_candidates: AC rectification and DC-bus conditioning stage
Phase current sensing and threshold-based protection
rationale: Top terms (brushless, motor, winding, voltage, phase winding, current, period) and titles dominated by 'CONTROL OF A BRUSHLESS MOTOR' / 'PERMANENT-MAGNET MOTOR' indicate the cluster centers on brushless PM motor control. Abstracts repeatedly describe exciting a winding with a (rectified) voltage, freewheeling when current exceeds a threshold, and using overrun/timeout periods—supporting modules for excitation/freewheeling control, current-threshold sensing, and an AC-to-rectified-voltage front end.

engineering_module_candidates: Rotor assembly (shaft, rotor core, magnet, impeller)
Stator assembly (stator core, C-shaped core variants)
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms (rotor, stator, rotor assembly, bearing, shaft, frame, stator core, impeller, magnet, adhesive) and title terms (brushless motor, compressor, turbomachine) consistently point to brushless motor construction. Representative titles ('BRUSHLESS MOTOR', 'COMPRESSOR', 'BRUSHLESS MOTOR AND STATOR THEREFOR') and abstracts repeatedly describe a rotor assembly with shaft/bearing/impeller/rotor core, a stator assembly (including C-shaped stator cores), and a frame housing them—often applied in compressors—directly supporting the module breakdown above.

suggested_cluster_label: Cleaning appliances: dental fluid-jet devices and floor tools for vacuum cleaners
engineering_module_candidates: Dental cleaning handpiece (handle + detachable cleaning tool)
Working-fluid delivery system (nozzle, moveable fluid conduit, burst delivery)
Floor tool attachment for vacuum cleaning appliances
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms split into two coherent sub-themes: (a) 'dental', 'working fluid', 'nozzle' (implied via abstracts), 'reservoir', 'conduit', 'handle' — clearly evidenced by repeated abstracts describing a dental cleaning appliance with a handle, detachable cleaning tool, nozzle delivering bursts of working fluid, moveable fluid conduit, and fluid reservoir; and (b) 'floor', 'floor tool', 'suction', 'vacuum cleaning appliance' from both TF-IDF and title terms ('floor tool', 'vacuum cleaning', 'tool vacuum', 'domestic appliance'), supporting a floor-tool/suction module for vacuum cleaners. Confidence is high for the dental fluid-delivery modules (directly quoted in abstracts) and moderate for the floor-tool/vacuum modules (supported by terms and titles but no abstract excerpts shown).

suggested_cluster_label: Wearable air purifiers and air treatment appliances
engineering_module_candidates: Motor-driven impeller / fan assembly for airflow generation
Wearable ear/head assembly with integrated speaker
Air purifier housing and airflow ducting
rationale: Top terms 'filter', 'air', 'airflow', 'fan', 'wearable', 'purifier', 'filter assembly' and titles like 'HEAD WEARABLE AIR PURIFIER' and 'WEARABLE AIR PURIFIER' point to wearable air purification devices. Abstracts explicitly describe a 'filter assembly', a 'motor-driven impeller for creating an airflow', and 'ear assembly' / 'speaker assembly' worn over the ear, supporting the filter, impeller/fan, and wearable ear-speaker modules. The presence of 'dryer', 'hand dryer', and 'drying' in top terms indicates a secondary sub-theme of drying/hand-dryer airflow products, though representative titles emphasize wearable purifiers, so confidence in the drying module is lower.
